about.loreDesignation: Web-Designer, Web-Slinger. Tomato, tomato. Wait, y'all are still on Web2? Oh, it's ghetto over here.
about.loreOrigin: I borrowed some tech from Kang No. 1337, and we don't have a designation for whatever Earth you're reading this from. Don't tell him though.
about.loreMotto: Something my folks told me long before I became a spida, so I etched it under my visor as a reminder.
about.originBody1p1: Pop culture was never just entertainment in my house. It was how my dad and I talked. He had his baseball cards and Magic: The Gathering collection. I had my Pokémon and Yu-Gi-Oh! decks. We'd spend hours comparing pulls, debating strategies, geeking out over whatever we were into that week. It wasn't about the cards. It was about having a language that was ours.
about.originBody1p2: Every Spider-Person has a canon event, something that shapes who they become. This was mine. The moment I learned that the things we love aren't just things. They're bridges. The Spidaverse exists because that bridge shouldn't stop at your living room.
about.originBody2p1: I lost my father to the pandemic. And in the years since, aunts, uncles, friends. People who shaped me in ways I'm still figuring out. Every Spider-Person loses someone. Uncle Ben. Aunt May. Captain Stacy. It's supposed to be the event that forges you, the thing the multiverse says you have to go through.
about.originBody2p2: But Spider-Man isn't a mask I wear to hide from that. It's a symbol I carry with me because of it. When things got heavy, I kept coming back to Peter Parker. Not because he had answers, but because he never stopped swinging. Fall down 7, get up 8. That resilience became something I needed to see, over and over again. The web is bigger than one person. And the people I've lost are woven into every strand of it.
about.originTitle3: Canon Event #003: The Leap of Faith
about.originBody3p1: In Into the Spider-Verse, Miles doesn't become Spider-Man by following the script. He breaks it. The multiverse tells him there are canon events he has to accept, and he says no. I'm choosing my own path. That's the version of Spider-Man that lives in my head.
about.originBody3p2: I wish I'd taken more leaps when my pops was around. I wanted to make him as proud of me as I am to have had him as a dad. But the thing about a leap of faith is that you don't get to pick the timing. You just have to jump when you're ready. This is that jump. Not perfect, not following anyone else's blueprint. Just swinging.
about.originBody3p3: If you're kind, passionate, and open-minded? Welcome to the web. It's yours too.
about.skillCommunityTagline: The web needs people in it
about.gadgetCategoryBadgesTagline: Tagging system for the multiverse catalog
about.webRatingDescription: Every piece of media gets a Web Rating out of 100, visualized as a spider web that fills based on the score. Not because I want to debate whether something deserves an 87 or a 91, but because sometimes you need to put a number on how much something mattered to you.
about.beliefText1: I write like I'm putting a friend onto something. Honest, but never just to be loud.
about.beliefText2: Not everything needs to be a masterpiece to be worth your time. Some things are comfort, some things challenge you. I try to meet all of it where it is.
about.beliefText3: This isn't a one-person operation. If something resonates, if you disagree, if you want to put me onto something I missed, that's the whole point.
